title: Metal shadow depth-clip clamp / depth read-write hazard / MSL autorelease leaks (M3.2)

history.note: Landed dc55008 (depth-clip clamp on shadow pass) + 73bbdac (1x1 far-cleared dummy depth instead of live scene depth attachment as shadow fallback) + e8d283a (@autoreleasepool around each MSL compile). NOT verified: renderer_parity scenes are facility_scissor/surface_sky_fog/world_attrs — none shadow-casting — so the parity smoke does not guard the shadow depth-clip/fallback; the autorelease leak is not pixel-observable at all. Needs a shadow-parity scene (+ leak/robustness assertion).

P1b: added the two GE007_NO_METAL_SHADOW_* negative-control flags (charter rule 4a): _DEPTH_CLAMP reverts dc55008 (shadow-pass MTLDepthClipModeClamp), _DUMMY_DEPTH reverts 73bbdac (cleared 1x1 nil-fallback vs the pre-fix s_scene_depth read-while-write hazard). Added tools/metal_shadow_clamp_regression.sh (ctest port_metal_shadow_clamp_regression, Metal-only, SKIPs off-Metal/headless) -- a REAL shadow-casting scene (Dam + GE007_SUN_SHADOW; shadow map 28.46% caster occupancy) that the cited renderer_parity smoke never exercised (facility_scissor/surface_sky_fog/world_attrs are shadow-free). HONEST DOWNGRADE landed->fix-in-progress: the fix cannot reach charter 4.3 (oracle-verify BOTH sides). The depth-clip clamp's only visible effect is near-plane pancaking; on the reachable deterministic Dam scene the shadow map is byte-IDENTICAL fix-on vs clamp-revert across radii 20..250 (measured casters min-depth 130/255 never reach the near plane at 0), so no both-sides pixel divergence is producible without a hand-crafted caster crossing the light near plane. The dummy-depth fallback is unreachable in normal play by design (73bbdac). The lane guards what IS assertable (shadow path live+non-trivial, fallback unreachable, both flags wired) and documents the inertness. Remains fix-in-progress pending a near-plane-pancaking scene (or a waiver). autorelease hygiene e8d283a is leak-only, no flag. Render-only proven: sim_invariance PASS + gfx_metal.mm executes only under GE007_RENDERER=metal (sim-hash lanes use GL).
